page: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the ESv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz, configured with 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports AVX and AVX-512 instructions. The server is equipped with 432.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 27.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 885 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, with no integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates that the E64-16s_v3 exhibits average multi-core and memory performance alongside weaker single-core capabilities. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth (reaching 104.14 GB/sec for uncached large block reads) rank in the middle 50% tier. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform at average levels, with Redis achieving 12.59 million operations per second.

The E64-16s_v3 is a constrained-core instance, meaning it limits the active vCPU count to 16 while maintaining the massive 432.0 GB memory footprint of a larger 64-vCPU host. This design provides high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads constrained by database software licensing models that charge per active CPU core, allowing users to maximize memory capacity without paying for unneeded CPU licenses. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-core processing speed and reduced overall compute density relative to the massive memory allocation.

This server is highly suited for memory-intensive database systems, such as Microsoft SQL Server or Oracle Database, where per-core licensing costs are a primary concern. It is also appropriate for large in-memory caches, enterprise resource planning (ERP) applications, and memory-bound data processing tasks that do not require high single-threaded CPU performance.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v3 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the ESv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores) and an exceptionally high memory capacity of 432.0 GB, alongside 885 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance is Premium Storage capable and lacks a GPU. Performance benchmarks place its multi-core processing and memory bandwidth in the average tier, while single-core performance is relatively weak. As a constrained-core instance, it offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for database engines and enterprise software where licensing is billed per core but memory demands are high. It is best suited for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v3 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 vCPUs, 432 GB RAM, and 885 GB local storage. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring per-core licensing optimization.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16s_v3 is an Intel-based memory-optimized server with 16 vCPUs, 432 GB RAM, and 885 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized, constrained-core virtual machine designed to maximize RAM capacity while minimizing per-core software licensing overhead.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 35 MB L3 cache
Massive 432.0 GB memory allocation providing 27.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
885 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Constrained-core architecture optimized for per-core software licensing
Best suited for memory-intensive databases and in-memory caching
